raw_response:
Full-mouth disinfections, also known as quadrants or hemi-arch procedures, typically aim to treat periodontal infections by targeting all areas affected. The interval between these treatments can vary based on several factors including the severity of infection and patient tolerance for discomfort from treatment. Given that this procedure often requires significant manipulation of tissues with potential post-treatment sensitivity, an adequate recovery time before repeat quadrant therapy seems reasonable.

Answer Choice C: 72 hours To allow sufficient healing and reduce the risk of exacerbating any existing conditions without prolonging unnecessary downtime, I would opt for option C (72 hours). This duration provides ample time for tissue regeneration while minimizing complications associated with frequent re-invasions during active inflammation periods. However, individual circumstances should always be evaluated to tailor appropriate intervals if necessary.
